title: Sunrun Q4 2023 Earnings Report Summary
text: Sunrun reported Q4 2023 revenue of $516.6 million, down 15% year-over-year. The company generated $11 million in cash in the quarter and reiterated positive cash generation outlook for 2024. Net loss attributable to common stockholders reached $350.1 million, partly due to $58.7 million non-cash charge related to Lunar Energy investment. The company achieved significant momentum in battery systems, installing 219.7 megawatt hours of storage capacity in Q4—a 154% year-over-year increase. Storage attachment rates surged to 45% of installations during quarter, with recent sales reaching approximately 48% nationally. CEO Mary Powell noted that Sunrun ended 2023 in a great position and started 2024 with incredible momentum. Customer additions reached 30,005 in Q4 with solar capacity installed of 227.1 megawatts. Net Subscriber Value reached $13,445, exceeding guidance with total customers at 933,275 as of year-end. Annual Recurring Revenue exceeded $1.3 billion. Strategic initiatives included launching customer renewal pilot program with encouraging results, partnering with Lowe's to staff over 260 retail locations, and expanding standalone storage offerings. The company strengthened its balance sheet by raising over $3 billion in capital during 2024.
